statement.en: The design of soft-actuated upper-body exoskeletons for movement assistance and physical therapy
statement.summary: This thesis presents the design, implementation, andvalidationof novel soft-actuatedexoskeletons formovementassistanceandrehabilita tion.Motivatedbytheneedtoenhancequalityoflifeforindividualswith neurologicalimpairments,suchasstrokesurvivorsandspinal-cord-injured individuals, thisworkexploreshybridassistivedevices thatmerge the comfort,compliance,andweightofsoftactuatorswiththestability,relia bility,andalignmentprovidedbyrigidstructures.Twokeysystemswere introduced: (1)ahighdegree-of-freedom(DOF) soft-actuatedarmand spineexoskeletonthatuseslightweightpolyethylenepneumaticactuators integrated intoa3D-printed frame, achievingover sevenDOFs includ ingforearmandshouldersupination/pronationmovements,easilycarried andmobile, capableof 3-dimensionalmovement. Experimental evalua tionsdemonstrate t

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Thesis dated 2026-04-25 (future date relative to knowledge cutoff) presents novel soft-exoskeleton design with experimental validation claimed but abstract truncated before results presentation. Status: provisional design work, not yet peer-reviewed publication. Formalization of mechanical/control systems is CLASS-A complete, but clinical efficacy claims require longitudinal validation (CLASS-B).

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 柔らかさと軽さの比が自由度を掛けると、回復への道が生まれる。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 剛性の脊椎から柔らかな腕が呼吸するように広がり、四肢の先端で新しい動きが咲く。
